req.title: Device data ingest (negative)
req.statement: A registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest; unregistered devices shall be rejected. Negative paths including permission denial, missing mandatory elements, and conflicting state transitions must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers FHIR device data ingest with multiple failure modes. I selected five tests: (1) EP positive — a fully valid registered device pushes a well-formed Observation and gets 201 with normalization applied; (2) NEG — an unregistered device cert yields 401; (3) NEG — a payload missing the mandatory 'subject' element is rejected with 422; (4) STC — a device already in 'completed' state attempts a conflicting transition back to 'active', which must be rejected; (5) BVA — a payload at exactly the maximum allowed Observation component count (boundary) is accepted. Together these cover EP, NEG, STC, BVA, and DT heuristics and satisfy the ≥1 positive, ≥1 negative, ≥1 boundary requirement.

tests.name: test_registered_device_ingest_valid_observation
preconditions: Device is registered in fhir-lite with a valid certificate
FHIR server is reachable at BASE_URL/Ingest
registered_device_cert fixture provides a valid mTLS cert pair
actions: Build a minimal valid FHIR Observation payload with resourceType, status, code, subject, and effectiveDateTime
POST the payload to /Ingest using the registered device certificate
expected: Response body contains 'meta.tag' indicating normalization was applied
trace: R-HLT-072: registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_registered_device_ingest_valid_observation(base_url, registered_device_cert):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-001"},
        "effectiveDateTime": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z",
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 72, "unit": "beats/min"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Ingest",
        json=payload,
        cert=registered_device_cert,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Response missing server-assigned id"
    tags = [t.get("code") for t in body.get("meta", {}).get("tag", [])]
    assert "normalized" in tags, f"Normalization tag not found; tags={tags}"

tests.name: test_unregistered_device_cert_rejected_401
preconditions: An unregistered (self-signed) certificate pair is available via unregistered_device_cert fixture
FHIR server enforces mTLS certificate validation against its device registry
actions: Build a structurally valid FHIR Observation payload
POST the payload to /Ingest using the unregistered device certificate
expected: Response body contains an error indicating certificate is invalid or device is unregistered
trace: R-HLT-072: unregistered devices shall be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unregistered_device_cert_rejected_401(base_url, unregistered_device_cert):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-001"},
        "effectiveDateTime": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z",
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 72, "unit": "beats/min"}
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Ingest",
        json=payload,
        cert=unregistered_device_cert,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    issue_codes = [i.get("code") for i in body.get("issue", [])]
    assert any(c in ("security", "forbidden", "unknown") for c in issue_codes), \
        f"Unexpected issue codes: {issue_codes}"

tests.name: test_missing_mandatory_subject_element_rejected
preconditions: Device is registered with a valid certificate (registered_device_cert fixture)
Server enforces FHIR Observation profile requiring 'subject'
actions: Build an Observation payload omitting the mandatory 'subject' element
POST the malformed payload to /Ingest with a valid registered cert
expected: Response OperationOutcome identifies 'subject' as the offending missing element
trace: R-HLT-072: missing mandatory elements must be tested
R-HLT-072: negative paths including missing mandatory elements
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_missing_mandatory_subject_element_rejected(base_url, registered_device_cert):
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "status": "final",
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4"}]},
        "effectiveDateTime": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z",
        "valueQuantity": {"value": 72, "unit": "beats/min"}
        # 'subject' intentionally omitted
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        f"{base_url}/Ingest",
        json=payload,
        cert=registered_device_cert,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422, f"Expected 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    diagnostics = " ".join(
        i.get("diagnostics", "") for i in body.get("issue", [])
    ).lower()
    assert "subject" in diagnostics, \
        f"Expected 'subject' in diagnostics, got: {diagnostics}"

tests.name: test_conflicting_state_transition_completed_to_active_rejected
preconditions: A previously ingested Observation with status 'final' exists on the server (observation_in_final_state fixture)
Device is registered with a valid certificate (registered_device_cert fixture)
Server enforces FHIR status state machine: 'final' -> 'registered' transition is forbidden
actions: Retrieve the existing Observation ID from the fixture
Build an update payload changing status from 'final' back to 'registered' (illegal backward transition)
PUT/POST the conflicting payload to /Ingest referencing the existing Observation
expected: Response OperationOutcome references invalid state transition
trace: R-HLT-072: conflicting state transitions must be tested
R-HLT-072: negative paths including conflicting state transitions
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_conflicting_state_transition_completed_to_active_rejected(
    base_url, registered_device_cert, observation_in_final_state
):
    obs_id = observation_in_final_state["id"]
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "Observation",
        "id": obs_id,
        "status": "registered",  # illegal: rolling back from 'final'
        "code": {"coding": [{"system": "http://loinc.org", "code": "8867-4"}]},
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-001"},
        "effectiveDateTime": "2024-01-15T10:00:00Z"
    }
    resp = requests.put(
        f"{base_url}/Ingest/{obs_id}",
        json=payload,
        cert=registered_device_cert,
        timeout=10
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (409, 422), \
        f"Expected 409 or 422, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    all_diag = " ".join(
        i.get("diagnostics", "") for i in body.get("issue", [])
    ).lower()
    assert any(kw in all_diag for kw in ("state", "transition", "status", "invalid")), \
        f"Expected state-transition error, got: {all_diag}"

tests.name: test_observation_at_max_component_count_boundary_accepted
preconditions: Device is registered with a valid certificate (registered_device_cert fixture)
MAX_COMPONENTS constant reflects the server's documented upper limit (e.g. 100 component entries)
Server accepts payloads up to but not exceeding MAX_COMPONENTS
actions: Build an Observation payload with exactly MAX_COMPONENTS component entries (boundary value)
POST the payload to /Ingest with a valid registered cert
expected: All MAX_COMPONENTS components are persisted in the stored resource
trace: R-HLT-072: boundary — exactly at maximum allowed component count
